instruction:
In January 2005, the population of California was 36.8 million and growing at an annual rate of 1.3\%. Assume that growth continues at the same rate.
(a) By how much will the population increase between 2005 and 2030? By [ANS] million (to the nearest 0.001 million) (b) By how much will the population increase between 2030 and 2055? By [ANS] million (to the nearest 0.001 million) (c) Explain how you can tell before doing the calculations which of the two answers in parts
(a) and (b) is larger. Select ALL statements in A-F which are true if more than one is possible. [ANS] A. The calculation in part (b) is larger since both increases are over 25 year periods, but since the graph of the function bends upward, the increase in the later time period is larger.  B. The calculation in part (b) is larger since the exponential function is concave up, and the average rate of change is increasing as time goes on.  C. The calculation in part (a) is larger since the graph of the function is concave down, and the average rate of change is decreasing as time goes on.  D. The calculation in part (a) is larger since the function is exponential, and exponential graphs grow faster at first, and then flatten.  E. The two answers are equal since the change in time from 2005 to 2030 is the same as the change in time from 2030 to 2055. Therefore the change in outputs will be the same.  F. None of the above
